arguments.content:
---
created_at: 2026-03-30T10:00:00+08:00
author: metaclaw_agent
status: done
---

# Team Onboarding Guide

**Welcome to the Orion Tech Backend Engineering Team!**

This guide will help you get up and running with ProjectX development environment and team practices.

---

## Day 1: Accounts & Access

### Required Accounts

| Service | Purpose | Request Access From |
|---------|---------|---------------------|
| GitHub (Organization) | Code repository | Alex |
| Slack (#backend-dev) | Team communication | HR |
| Figma | UI/UX reference | Chen |
| Jira | Sprint planning | PM |
| AWS Console | Cloud resources | Casey |
| Docker Hub | Container registry | Casey |

### Repository Access

You need access to these repositories:
- `projectx-api` — Main backend API
- `projectx-docs` — Documentation
- `projectx-infrastructure` — Terraform/K8s configs

---

## Day 1-2: Environment Setup

### Prerequisites Installation

```bash
# macOS (using Homebrew)
brew install node@18 postgresql@14 redis
brew install --cask docker

# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt update
sudo apt install nodejs npm postgresql redis-server
sudo apt install docker.io docker-compose
```

Required versions:
- **Node.js**: 18.x or higher
- **PostgreSQL**: 14.x or higher
- **Redis**: 6.x or higher
- **Docker**: Latest stable
- **npm**: 9.x or higher

### Project Setup

```bash
# 1. Clone the main repository
git clone https://github.com/oriontech/projectx-api.git
cd projectx-api

# 2. Install dependencies
npm install

# 3. Copy environment configuration
cp .env.example .env.development

# 4. Edit .env.development with your local settings
# Ask Casey for development database credentials

# 5. Initialize database
npm run db:create
npm run db:migrate
npm run db:seed:dev

# 6. Start development server
npm run dev
```

Verify setup: `curl http://localhost:3000/health` should return `{"status":"ok"}`

### Docker Setup (Alternative)

```bash
# Run full stack with Docker Compose
docker-compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml up -d

# View logs
docker-compose -f docker-compose.dev.yml logs -f api
```

---

## Day 2-3: Code Conventions

### Project Structure

```
projectx-api/
├── src/
│   ├── config/          # Configuration files
│   ├── controllers/     # Route controllers
│   ├── middleware/      # Express middleware
│   ├── models/          # Database models
│   ├── routes/          # API route definitions
│   ├── services/        # Business logic
│   ├── utils/           # Utility functions
│   └── validators/      # Input validation
├── tests/
│   ├── unit/            # Unit tests
│   ├── integration/     # Integration tests
│   └── e2e/             # End-to-end tests
├── docs/                # Markdown documentation
├── scripts/             # Utility scripts
└── migrations/          # Database migrations
```

### Naming Conventions

| Type | Convention | Example |
|------|------------|---------|
| Files | kebab-case | `user-controller.js` |
| Classes | PascalCase | `UserController` |
| Functions/Variables | camelCase | `getUserById` |
| Constants | UPPER_SNAKE_CASE | `MAX_RETRY_COUNT` |
| Database Tables | snake_case, plural | `users`, `payment_transactions` |
| API Endpoints | kebab-case | `/api/v2/user-profiles` |

### Code Style

We use **ESLint + Prettier** with the following rules:

```javascript
// 2-space indentation
function example() {
  if (condition) {
    doSomething();
  }
}

// Single quotes for strings
const message = 'Hello world';

// Trailing commas in multi-line objects
const config = {
  host: 'localhost',
  port: 3000,
};

// Async/await preferred over callbacks
const user = await User.findById(id);
```

Run linting: `npm run lint`  
Auto-fix issues: `npm run lint:fix`

### Git Workflow

```bash
# 1. Create feature branch from main
git checkout main
git pull origin main
git checkout -b feature/123-short-description

# 2. Make commits with conventional messages
git commit -m "feat: add user profile endpoints"
git commit -m "fix: correct pagination offset calculation"
git commit -m "docs: update API authentication guide"

# 3. Push and create PR
git push -u origin feature/123-short-description
```

**Commit Message Format:**
- `feat:` New feature
- `fix:` Bug fix
- `docs:` Documentation changes
- `test:` Adding or updating tests
- `refactor:` Code refactoring
- `chore:` Maintenance tasks

### Documentation Standards

All Markdown files must include frontmatter:

```yaml
---
created_at: 2026-03-30T10:00:00+08:00
author: your_github_username
status: in_progress
---
```

Filename format: `YYYYMMDD_description.md`

---

## Common Commands Reference

### Development

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run dev` | Start development server with hot reload |
| `npm run build` | Build for production |
| `npm start` | Start production server |
| `npm run db:migrate` | Run database migrations |
| `npm run db:rollback` | Rollback last migration |
| `npm run db:seed` | Seed database with test data |
| `npm run db:reset` | Reset database (caution: deletes data) |

### Testing

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm test` | Run unit tests |
| `npm run test:watch` | Run tests in watch mode |
| `npm run test:integration` | Run integration tests |
| `npm run test:e2e` | Run end-to-end tests |
| `npm run test:coverage` | Generate coverage report |

### Code Quality

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `npm run lint` | Check code style |
| `npm run lint:fix` | Auto-fix style issues |
| `npm run format` | Format code with Prettier |
| `npm run type-check` | Run TypeScript type checking |

### Docker

| Command | Description |
|---------|-------------|
| `docker-compose up -d` | Start all services |
| `docker-compose down` | Stop all services |
| `docker-compose logs -f` | Follow logs |
| `docker-compose build` | Rebuild containers |

### API Testing (Local)

```bash
# Health check
curl http://localhost:3000/health

# Authenticate
curl -X POST http://localhost:3000/api/v2/auth/login \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email":"dev@oriontech.com","password":"test123"}'

# Get users (with auth token)
curl http://localhost:3000/api/v2/users \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>"
```

---

## Key Resources

### Documentation

| Resource | Location | Description |
|----------|----------|-------------|
| API Reference | `/docs/api/` | OpenAPI-generated docs |
| Architecture | `/docs/architecture/` | System design docs |
| Runbooks | `/docs/runbooks/` | Operational procedures |
| ADRs | `/docs/decisions/` | Architecture decision records |

### Team Contacts

| Role | Name | Slack Handle |
|------|------|--------------|
| Tech Lead | Alex | @alex.zhang |
| Backend Engineer | Bai | @bai.wei |
| DevOps Engineer | Casey | @casey.wu |
| QA Engineer | Dana | @dana.liu |

### Useful Slack Channels

- `#backend-dev` — Daily team communication
- `#backend-alerts` — Production alerts
- `#sprint-8-updates` — Sprint progress updates
- `#docs-review` — Documentation collaboration

---

## First Week Checklist

- [ ] Complete access requests (Day 1)
- [ ] Set up local development environment (Day 1-2)
- [ ] Attend team standup (Daily, 9:00 AM)
- [ ] Complete first code review with mentor (Day 2-3)
- [ ] Submit first PR (Day 3-4)
- [ ] Read ADR-001 (API Versioning) (Day 2)
- [ ] Review Sprint 8 goals (Day 2)
- [ ] Schedule 1:1 with Alex (Week 1)

---

## Getting Help

1. **Technical questions:** Post in #backend-dev with context
2. **Code review:** Tag @alex.zhang for backend architecture questions
3. **Environment issues:** DM @casey.wu
4. **Process questions:** Check this guide first, then ask Alex

Welcome to the team! 🚀

---

*Guide version: 1.0*  
*Last updated: March 30, 2026*  
*Maintained by: Backend Engineering Team*
